compiled_state.title: As a fix-bug workflow, I can detect when a proposed fix reverses an intentional change or introduces new dependencies
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add Gate 2a (reversal check) and Gate 2d (new dependency/architecture check) to fix-bug's post-investigation workflow. Gate 2a checks git history to detect if the proposed fix undoes a recent intentional change. Gate 2d checks if the fix introduces dependencies or architectural patterns not already present in the codebase.
**Why**: Fixes that reverse intentional design decisions or introduce unreviewed dependencies cause architectural drift. These gates catch the two most structural risk categories before implementation.
**Scope**:
- IN: Gate 2a reversal detection via git log on affected files; revert-of-revert pattern recognition in commit messages; Gate 2d dependency novelty check (new imports/packages not in project) and architecture novelty check (patterns not already used); both gates are PRIMARY SIGNALS
- OUT: Blast radius analysis (Story 5); test regression analysis (Story 4); escalation routing (Story 6)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a fix that undoes a recent intentional change (detected via git history) fires Gate 2a as a primary signal
  ← Satisfies: "A fix that introduces a runtime or dev dependency not already in the project, or introduces an architectural pattern not already used in the codebase, is flagged"
- When this story is complete, revert-of-revert patterns in commit messages are recognized and do not trigger Gate 2a
  ← Satisfies: approach clarification on revert-of-revert handling
- When this story is complete, a fix that introduces a new runtime/dev dependency or novel architectural pattern fires Gate 2d as a primary signal
  ← Satisfies: "A fix that introduces a runtime or dev dependency not already in the project...is flagged for user dialog"
- When this story is complete, reusing an existing codebase pattern (e.g., adding a sub-agent dispatch where others already exist) does not trigger Gate 2d
  ← Satisfies: "Reusing an existing codebase pattern does not trigger this gate"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← TDD Done-of-Done

comments.body: Consideration: [Scope] Verify whether reversal detection (Gate 2a) also needs intent-search suppression — an intentional revert classified as intent-aligned by Gate 1a should not be flagged. If suppression is needed, add a dependency on Story 1.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: All 8 child tasks completed: reversal check, dependency check scripts, RED tests, and Gates 2a/2d SKILL.md integration.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
